mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month growth, creating negative real rates that incentivize equity risk-taking and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-crisis zero-rate environment with reflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during zero-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) decelerates below 100,000 net new jobs, signaling weakening labor demand and triggering recession fears that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < 100,000 for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with early labor market softening signals
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite payroll deceleration, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) show sustained month-over-month growth above 0.3%, indicating robust consumer demand that supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth fails to predict positive returns in > 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.5%, indicating simultaneous supply contraction and labor market deterioration that signals stagflation risk and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: supply-side shock with labor market stress
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of INDPRO decline and UNRATE rise does not significantly predict negative returns (interaction p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline month-over-month below 1,100 thousand units while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2.5% annualized, indicating weakening residential investment and subdued aggregate demand that compress equity multiples and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1,100 thousand AND GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:August 2015 HOUST at 1,174 thousand, GDPC1 at 16,270.4 billion
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with housing market deceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the joint HOUST-GDPC1 signal is < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.5% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) shows month-over-month growth below 150,000, indicating labor market deterioration that signals weakening economic momentum and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:August 2015 UNRATE at 5.3%, PAYEMS at 142,071 thousand
regime_hint: labor market inflection point with dual deterioration signals
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 62% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between UNRATE and PAYEMS growth does not significantly predict negative returns (p > 0.08).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates to year-over-year growth above 2.5% while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 0.50%, indicating monetary policy is behind the inflation curve and will eventually tighten, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as markets price in future rate increases.
regime_hint: monetary policy normalization cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the predictive power of the inflation-rate gap deteriorates to Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) shows sustained month-over-month growth above 0.2% while real retail sales (RSAFS) grow above 0.3%, indicating broad-based economic expansion across supply and demand that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > 0.2% AND RSAFS month-over-month change > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:August 2015 INDPRO at 107.477, RSAFS at 446,477 million
regime_hint: synchronized expansion across supply and demand sectors
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of INDPRO and RSAFS growth does not significantly predict positive returns (interaction p-value > 0.10).
